text:
---
name: local-browser-relaunch
description: Cleanly stop and relaunch this repo's local headed pipeline browser session. Use when the user asks to relaunch, restart, or close-and-reopen the local browser, or when an older `live-browser` session is hanging, serving stale code, or still holding ports `5057` / `9223`.
---

# Local Browser Relaunch

Run the bundled restart script from the repo root. It reads `local testing/runtime/live-browser-session.json`, stops the recorded session process tree if present, force-cleans any orphan listeners on the app port (`5057`) and Chromium debug port (`9223`), waits briefly, and launches the standard `local testing/launch-live-browser.js` flow again.

## Command

```powershell
node .agents/skills/local-browser-relaunch/scripts/relaunch-live-browser.js
```

Pass through any normal live-browser arguments after `--`, for example:

```powershell
node .agents/skills/local-browser-relaunch/scripts/relaunch-live-browser.js -- --path /pipeline
```

## Notes

- Keep artifacts inside `local testing/runtime/`.
- Prefer this skill over ad-hoc `taskkill` commands so the restart path stays consistent with the standard launcher.
- This skill is the right fix when the browser appears to be serving stale frontend/backend code after a restart; it explicitly clears orphan port owners before relaunching.
- If the session file is missing, the script skips the stop step and just launches a fresh browser.
- After relaunch, verify the app is healthy at `http://127.0.0.1:5057/login` and use the new `live-browser-session.json` plus runtime logs as the source of truth.
